summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #3: nodes in BulkEdit's Category mode were displayed in array index order (canvas insertion order) instead of canvas position order (TD: top-to-bottom, LR: left-to-right).
summary.outcome: Added position-based sorting for both nodes and edges in BulkEdit Category mode, committed as c002914 closing issue #3. Edges sort by source node position first, then target node position.

repo: BulkEdit has two modes: Category mode (nodes/edges displayed separately) and Flow mode (topological sort already applied).
The store has a `direction` field with values 'TD' or 'LR' used across the app for layout direction.
Flow mode already had proper topological sorting; only Category mode was missing position-based sorting.
Node positions are available on node objects as x/y coordinates that reflect canvas placement.

code.finding: Category mode items builder needed sortNodesByPosition and sortEdgesByPosition helpers. Edge sort uses editableNodes (not filteredNodes) as position reference to avoid sort order breaking when source nodes are filtered out.

workflow: Dev server startup checked via `curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000` before starting to avoid duplicate processes.
Cloudflare tunnel via `cloudflared tunnel --url http://localhost:3000` provides temporary trycloudflare.com URLs for external preview.
Type check + test run used as CI gate before committing; 70 tests passed.
Code review agent caught a bug (sortEdgesByPosition referencing filteredNodes instead of editableNodes) that was fixed before commit.

friction: Initial review agent caught a correctness bug post-implementation (edge sort referencing filteredNodes instead of editableNodes), requiring an extra edit-check-test cycle.
Multiple sequential reads of BulkEditTable.tsx were needed during implementation, suggesting the file is large enough to require partial reads.
open_items: Issues #1, #2, and #4 remain open: LR bug in component edit screen, TD-form placement bug when re-editing LR components, and beta popup on launch.
Edge sorting by target node position was added as a follow-up after initial commit — worth verifying the UX feels natural for complex graphs with many edges sharing the same source.
